behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from recommendation results regardless of relevance score
behaviors.justification: A real failure is hidden data freshness or race conditions: a product cached as in-stock when it's actually out-of-stock, or a stock status update between ranking and filtering. Unit tests with mock data miss these scenarios. Integration testing requires actual DB operations to verify stock state is correctly fetched and applied, including edge cases where stock changes concurrently.
behaviors.test_description: Create product with stock=true in DB and request recommendations. Verify product is included. Update DB to stock=false and request again immediately. Verify product is excluded. Repeat 10 times to catch race conditions where cache staleness causes inconsistent results across requests.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings
behaviors.justification: The real failure is user preferences not being loaded, stale cached preferences, or wrong preferences for the user. Unit tests with mock preferences hide data loading failures. Integration testing requires loading actual user preferences from storage, applying the filter to real product data, and verifying consistency when preferences change.
behaviors.test_description: Create user with 3 blocked categories in settings. Request recommendations. Verify no products in blocked categories appear. Update user settings to block a different category. Request again. Verify newly blocked category is excluded and previously blocked category now appears (if available).

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal
behaviors.justification: While the ranking formula itself could be unit-tested, real failures include: browsing history not being loaded correctly, stale browsing data in cache, or products not being matched to browsing history properly. A unit mock that provides clean data misses these integration failures. Integration testing verifies that browsing history is fetched from storage, correctly matched to products, and produces higher ranks.
behaviors.test_description: User browses product A, B, C. Clear browsing data and add product A to cache of what user viewed. Request recommendations and verify product A ranks higher than product D (never browsed). Run twice to verify consistency. Add product D to browsing history and request again, verifying product D now ranks higher.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if underlying data hasn't changed
behaviors.justification: Unit tests with mock arrays can hide real ordering failures: incorrect OFFSET/LIMIT in DB queries, unstable sort orders with actual data, non-deterministic iteration, or cursor inconsistency between paginated requests. Integration testing with a real database is required to verify correct offset calculation, stable ordering across multiple requests, and data consistency between requests.
behaviors.test_description: Load 250 products into test DB with stable sort key. Request page 1 (results 1-50) and page 2 (results 51-100). Repeat page 2 request 5 times and verify all 5 responses are byte-identical. Request page 1 again to verify offset 0 is correct. Request page 3 and verify no overlap with pages 1-2.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble
behaviors.justification: Unit tests with mock catalogs can hide real failures: insufficient unbrowsed categories in actual catalog, serendipity logic never triggering, or category classification mismatches between browsing history and product catalog. Integration testing with a realistic product catalog distribution is needed to verify serendipity selection works across varied category counts and that at least one unbrowsed product appears consistently.
behaviors.test_description: Create user with browsing history in 5 product categories. Product catalog has 20 categories total (15 unbrowsed). Request recommendations 20 times. Verify every result set includes at least one product from an unbrowsed category. Calculate histogram of unbrowsed categories selected and verify fairness (no category always/never picked).

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set
behaviors.justification: The real failure is a logic branching issue: if-statement not checking for empty history, or falling back to empty set instead of popularity. This can be caught at unit level with mock data showing both the empty-history case and popularity data. The risk of a unit mock missing the failure is low because the failure is in control flow (does it check history?) not in data consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test: mock user with empty browsing_history and empty purchase_history. Mock popularity rankings for products. Call recommendation function. Verify returned recommendations come from popularity rankings, not from history-based ranking. Verify result is not empty.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with
behaviors.justification: Unit tests with mock storage miss real failures: position calculated wrong relative to pagination, timestamp precision issues, click storage failures, or race conditions in concurrent clicks. Integration testing with actual recommendation generation and storage is required to verify position maps correctly (considering pagination offset), timestamps are accurate, and all clicks are persisted.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations for page 2 (offset 50). User clicks product at position 0 in returned list (actual DB position 50). Verify click event stored with product_id=<correct>, position=50, timestamp within 1 second of request. Simulate rapid clicks on multiple positions and verify all recorded without loss or duplication.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products
behaviors.justification: Unit and integration tests with small datasets (100s-1000s of products) can hide algorithmic complexity or query performance issues that emerge only at scale. Real production failure occurs when actual infrastructure (DB with 100k products, network I/O) reveals O(n²) algorithms, missing indexes, or N+1 queries. System-level testing with realistic data scale and infrastructure is required.
behaviors.test_description: Load real test DB with 100,000 products with realistic distribution across categories, prices, and popularity. Run recommendation requests for 50 different users with varied browsing histories. Measure response time for each request. Verify 99th percentile latency is under 200ms. Repeat with cache cold to catch worst-case scenarios.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests
behaviors.justification: Real failures include: purchase history not being loaded after purchase is recorded, cache not invalidated, stale purchase data used in ranking, or race conditions between purchase recording and recommendation ranking. Unit tests with mock purchase data hide data consistency failures. Integration testing requires recording a purchase, verifying cache invalidation, reloading purchase history, and confirming ranking changes.
behaviors.test_description: User purchases product P, which was in top 5 recommendations. Request recommendations again. Verify P is no longer in top 5 (or removed entirely). Request again multiple times to verify deprioritization persists. Update product to deprioritized status in DB directly and verify ranking immediately reflects change.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list
behaviors.justification: Unit tests with mock data can hide subtle non-determinism: hash map iteration order in some languages, floating-point comparison precision with actual data, non-deterministic tie-breaking, or timestamp-based sorting that varies milliseconds between requests. Integration testing with actual data storage and multiple sequential requests is required to verify byte-identical results.
behaviors.test_description: Request recommendations for a user. Store full response JSON. Wait 5 seconds. Request recommendations again for the same user (no intervening activity). Verify response JSON is identical (products in same order, same scores). Repeat cycle 10 times to catch intermittent non-determinism. Include products with tied scores to verify tie-breaking is stable.
